CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Continued To Dec-11-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE UNIVERSITY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Golden Gate University's demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are adjudged as follows.

The parties agree the Golden Gate University Alumni Association lacks standing to sue. (Memo. 10:6-15.; Opp. 5:27-28.) Demurrer sustained with prejudice.

The parties agree plaintiffs were required to get leave of court before adding a count for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, but did not do so. (Memo. 10:16-26; Opp. 5:28-29.) Demurrer sustained; plaintiffs may move to add this count.

The parties agree only plaintiff Danielle Morvan states a claim for promissory estoppel; indeed, hers is the only plaintiff's name stated in this count. (Memo. 11:2-13; Opp. 6:21-23; FAC 24:10-27:17.) Demurrer overruled.

Like other species of fraud, promissory fraud must be pled with specificity: "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representation were tendered." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) The FAC includes allegations that, with others, might be knit into a promissory fraud claim, but a coherent specific whole has yet to be pled. The same is true of the false advertising and UCL claims, which plaintiffs tie to each other and to the fraud claim. (Opp. 8:1, 25; FAC 34:22-25, 35:21-25.) Demurrers sustained with leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2024 Continued To Oct-30-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Injunctive Relief And/Or Specific Performance
Off Calendar Aug-26-2024 Continued To Sep-13-24 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Injunctive Relief And/Or Specific Performance
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was specially set at 1:30 p.m. before Judge Richard B. Ulmer.

Appearances:
Ryan Griffith, Esq. of Ryan Griffith Law P.C., Tel. 818.383.7348, appearing in person for Plaintiffs Stephen Downs, et al;
Mark Posard, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, appearing via Zoom for Defendant Golden Gate University.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFFS STEPHEN DOWNS, NATHAN LIMCOLIOC, YVONNE ULIBARRI, DANIELLE MORVAN, AND GOLDEN GATE UNIVERSITY ALUMNI ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND/OR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the matter is taken under submission.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. RYAN GRIFFITH, Esq. of Bay Area Receivership Group (tel: 510-995-0158) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MARK POSARD, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (tel: 415-986-5900) for Defendant Golden Gate University.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE UNIVERSITY's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Golden Gate University's demurrers to the complaint are all sustained with leave to amend.

Count 1 adequately pleads most elements of contract breach: existence of a contract, breach and damages, but does not plead plaintiffs' performance or excuse. (Cmplt. 10:6-11:23.) That said, defendant here demands particularity of pleading that California law does not. For example, the complaint's reader can tell whether the alleged contracts are written, oral or implied. They could turn out not to be contracts at all, but this is the pleading stage.

Count 2 fails to plead promissory estoppel. Built largely on "expectations" (Cmplt. 12:5, 9), this claim recites but one promise (id. at 12:8-9) and it is not "clear and unambiguous" as plaintiffs concede it must be. (Opp. 9:25.) Moreover, the reliance is not pled to be on the putative promise. (Cmplt. 12:13-14, 21-22.)

Count 3 fails to plead breach of fiduciary duty, because it pleads no fiduciary relationship. The complaint itself concedes the notion that a university owes students a fiduciary duty is dubious (13:6-7), and the opposition cites no case that has ever so held (much less that a law school owes law students a fiduciary duty). (Id. at 10:16-11:16; see also Zumbrun v. University of Southern California (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 1, 13 [no fiduciary duty in university-student context]; Kashmiri v. Regents of University of California (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 809 [fiduciary duty not discussed].)

Count 4 fails to plead fraud with the required particularity. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645 ["how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered"].) Moreover, Count 4 seeks to plead two causes of action, but each cause must be separately stated and numbered. (CRC 2.112.)
Count 5 seeks to plead two causes of action, but each cause must be separately stated and numbered. (CRC 2.112.)

Injunctive relief and appointment of a receiver (counts 6 and 7) are remedies, not causes of action as the complaint wrongly asserts. (Id. at 16:8, 19:7.)

The opposition does not address defendant's lack-of-standing demurrer.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. RYAN GRIFFITH, Esq. of Bay Area Receivership Group (tel: 510-995-0158) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MARK POSARD, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (tel: 415-986-5900) for Defendant Golden Gate University.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF STEPHEN DOWNS, NATHAN LIMCOLIOC, YVONNE ULIBARRI, DANIELLE MORVAN AND GOLDEN GATE UNIVERSITY ALUMNI ASSOCIATION's Motion For Injunctive Relief.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

As their complaint states (16:14-15), plaintiffs' claims' likelihood of success is the first consideration on a preliminary injunction motion. Given today's results on defendant's demurrers, it is not yet known what those claims may be, so plaintiffs' motion must be denied without prejudice.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
